business.headline: Indians are buying homes with money they haven't earned yetAs home prices race ahead of incomes, more Indians are stretching EMIs into retirement, cutting savings and relying on decades of future earnings to keep the dream of homeownership alive. This is the story of how India's housing dream is increasingly being financed by tomorrow's income.

Petrol, diesel, ATF export duty cut: What changes from June 1The government has cut export duties on petrol, diesel and aviation turbine fuel for the fortnight from June 1. The move follows its price-linked review mechanism, while domestic fuel excise and retail rates remain unchanged.

CNG prices hiked by Rs 2 per kg in Mumbai, Thane, Navi MumbaiThis is the second hike in less than a month, following an earlier revision on May 14. The cumulative Rs 4 rise is expected to impact daily commuters, taxi operators and commercial vehicle users who rely heavily on CNG.

Illness, insurance and the cost of timingWhat happens when a chronic illness arrives before health insurance? One Multiple Sclerosis patient's journey highlights the challenges of getting coverage for pre-existing diseases in India.

Will Kwality Wall's big dairy push help it compete better in India?The company is making changes across products, pricing, factories and supply systems. Around half of Kwality Wall's products are expected to be dairy-based this year, while most of the shift will be completed next year.

Could Delhi HC's Hindware verdict reshape Google Ads and digital marketing in India?The Delhi High Court has ruled that Google cannot allow rival companies to bid on the trademarked keyword "HINDWARE" through its advertising platform.

Sensex, Nifty crash over inflation concern on poor rain forecastThe S&P BSE Sensex plunged 1,092.06 points, or 1.44%, to close at 74,775.74, while the NSE Nifty50 dropped 359.40 points, or 1.50%, to settle at 23,547.75.

Absolut Vodka maker battles Delhi ban as Rs 3,000 crore tax dispute deepensThe Delhi High Court has rejected Pernod Ricard's plea to resume liquor sales in the capital. The order adds to a separate customs dispute that has put nearly Rs 3,000 crore at stake.

How Gen Z and women buyers are turning India into the 2nd-largest diamond marketWhat if diamonds were no longer saved for "special occasions"? Across India, Gen Z shoppers and self-buying women are increasingly choosing diamonds as everyday luxury, a shift that is helping the country emerge as the world's second-largest diamond market.

Are India's stock investors overtaxed? Vijay Kedia thinks so, offers 3 solutionsVeteran investor Vijay Kedia has urged the Finance Ministry that the country's tax policy should reward long-term wealth creation instead of burdening those who provide patient capital to businesses.

From rising prices to rupee pressure: 5 key takeaways from RBI's annual reportIndia's economy remains strong, but challenges are growing. Slower growth, rising inflation risks and geopolitical tensions may test the economy in the coming months.

A war away, but not far away: RBI's warning for India's economyFor all the talk around India's strong economic growth, resilient consumers and healthy banks, the RBI's latest assessment contains a clear message: the biggest risks to the country's growth story are increasingly coming from outside its borders.

Under-construction or ready-to-move home: Which saves me more money in the long run?Buying a home is exciting, but one question often leaves buyers confused: should you go for a cheaper under-construction property or pay more for a ready-to-move home? Let's have a look.

Inspection Raj in the service of employers evading complianceIn the ninth part of this series on labour laws and Inspector Raj, this article examines how reduced scrutiny in the name of "ease of doing business" may create space for wilful labour law violations.

Why Delta Corp share price crashed 16% todayDelta Corp shares slid 16% after the Supreme Court upheld the government's retrospective 28 per cent GST levy on online gaming companies. The ruling has intensified concerns over large tax demands and compliance costs across the sector.

8th Pay Commission: Pensioners seek option to choose between OPS, NPS, UPSEmployee and pensioner groups have urged the 8th Pay Commission to let government staff choose between OPS, NPS and UPS. The demand has put retirement security and clarity over post-retirement income at the centre of the wider pay panel discussions.

Wipro jumps 5%, lifts IT stocks as AI deal sparks sector rallyWipro shares: Wipro stock gained as much as 4.76% during the day to Rs 211.20, while its American Depositary Receipts (ADRs) listed in the US had jumped more than 18% overnight.

IT stocks lead gains as Sensex, Nifty open higher on easing oil pricesThe S&P BSE Sensex rose 98.09 points, or 0.13%, to 75,965.89 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 16.20 points, or 0.07%, to 23,923.35, as of 9:25 am.

SpaceX IPO: How Elon Musk's company is targeting a $1.8 trillion valuationSpaceX is discussing a valuation of about $1.8 trillion and a potential $75 billion raise ahead of its IPO. The filing shows the company is asking investors to see it as an AI infrastructure and space technology business.

Why India may be taking risky bet with $500 billion US dealMarco Rubio said in New Delhi that India had committed to buying $500 billion of American goods over five years. The remark has intensified scrutiny of whether the proposed arrangement offers India enough return.

entertainment.headline: Krystle D'Souza shares pic with man amid dating rumours with Desi Bling's AP Actor Krystle D'Souza sparked fresh dating rumours after sharing a photo holding a mystery man's hand. The post comes days after her viral party clip with Desi Bling's AP set social media buzzing.

The real horror in Barker's Obsession isn't supernatural; it's male entitlement Curry Barker's Obsession follows Bear's wish to make Nikki love him above all else. The film uses that supernatural premise to probe consent, agency and self-serving desire.

Kartik Aaryan 'absolutely obsessed' with Curry Barker's Obsession: Don't miss this Actor Kartik Aaryan praised Curry Barker's supernatural horror film Obsession on social media and urged fans not to miss it.

Shiva Rajkumar says he nearly quit Peddi during cancer treatment At the Chennai promotions for Peddi, Shiva Rajkumar revealed that he almost quit the film after being diagnosed with cancer and travelling to the US for treatment. He said the team chose to wait for his return instead of recasting the role, making the project a deeply personal journey for him.

Jana Nayagan director on film delay: I don't have the courage to reveal the truth Director H Vinoth gave a cryptic answer when asked about Jana Nayagan's release during a promotional interview. Touted to be Thalapathy Vijay's final film, Jana Nayagan is rumoured to hit theatres on the actor-politician's 52nd birthday in June.

Director Sanjay Gupta says underage children are watching A-rated Obsession Director Sanjay Gupta said he saw several underage viewers at a packed screening of A-certified film Obsession. The psychological horror thriller released in India on May 29.

Grateful to do life with you: Naga Chaitanya's loved-up birthday wish for Sobhita Naga Chaitanya had just one person on his mind on Sunday – Sobhita Dhulipala. The actor celebrated her 34th birthday with candid memories and a heartfelt note that quickly won over fans.

Mahima Chaudhry amused by daughter Arina's resemblance to Off Campus's Mika Abdalla Actor Mahima Chaudhry said viewers comparing daughter Arina to Off Campus actor Mika Abdalla had flooded her phone with messages. The resemblance has even led some people to ask if Arina has already made her acting debut.

CM Vijay, Trisha visit grieving Ajith Kumar after mother Mohini Mani's death CM Vijay and actor Trisha Krishnan visited actor Ajith Kumar's Chennai home after the death of his mother, Mohini Mani. The family matriarch died on Saturday at 85.

Dhurandhar high or Don 3 row: With Ranveer Singh it's all possible, no surprises Ranveer Singh does not behave like a conventional superstar. Amid public personas crafted for perfection, his motto seems: This is me, take it or leave it. With the actor, anything is possible.

Naga Chaitanya approaches Delhi court against identity misuse, gets interim relief Actor Naga Chaitanya approached the Delhi High Court seeking protection against false and defamatory online content misusing his identity, including AI-generated material. The court has thus issued an interim order safeguarding his personality rights.

Ghooskhor Pandat row: Manoj Bajpayee says trolls abused him, targeted his family Actor Manoj Bajpayee said the backlash over Ghooskhor Pandat brought threats, abuse and attacks on his family. He said the team apologised quickly, agreed to change the title, but felt people reacted without knowing the film's subject.

Brown trailer: Karisma Kapoor fights inner turmoil while solving a murder The trailer of Brown introduces Karisma Kapoor as a troubled police officer investigating a teenager's murder in Kolkata. The Zee5 series also stars Jisshu Sengupta and Soni Razdan, among others.

From Tejasswi's 'shut up' to Karan's proposal: Inside the making of Desi Bling Desi Bling producer Mazen Laham reveals how the Netflix reality show was cast and whether the drama was scripted. He also shared behind-the-scenes details of Tejasswi's viral 'shut uppp' moment and Karan Kundrra's proposal.

Why Arun Govil believes Ranbir Kapoor's Ramayana faces an uphill battle Actor Arun Govil said his long association with Lord Rama shaped reactions to Ranbir Kapoor's first Ramayana glimpse. He also explained why he hesitated before joining the film as Lord Dasharath.

Ram Charan congratulates Tamil Nadu CM Vijay: You left stardom, career at peak Actor Ram Charan congratulated actor-turned-politician Vijay on becoming the Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu, calling it a proud moment for the film industry and the people of Tamil Nadu. He said Vijay had stepped away from stardom and his career at its peak.

Obsession on OTT: When, where to watch horror sleeper hit everyone's talking about Michael Johnston and Inde Navarrette's psychological horror thriller has emerged as a surprise box-office success. Here's when the film will head to OTT after its theatrical run.

Ram Charan on how Peddi changed him as an actor and person In an exclusive interview with India Today, Ram Charan said Peddi allowed him to represent people who need voice and recognition. He called it his toughest role and revealed he pushed through a worsening injury during the wrestling shoot.

Jackie Shroff joins Sanjay Dutt in Khalnayak Returns, confirms role Jackie Shroff has confirmed that he will return for Khalnayak Returns, reuniting with Sanjay Dutt in the sequel to the 1993 cult hit. The veteran actor said he has already spoken to Subhash Ghai, the producer and Dutt about the project.

general news.headline: Who attacked Abhishek Banerjee? TMC, BJP pin blame on each otherThe attack on Abhishek Banerjee has triggered a political confrontation between TMC and BJP, with both parties trading allegations over responsibility. While TMC claims BJP-backed mob involvement and cites the presence of a local BJP functionary, the BJP counters that internal rivalries within the TMC may have driven the incident.

CBSE's U-turn, admits security gaps after online marking portal hacked twiceAmid the ongoing controversy surrounding its OSM system, CBSE has issued its first public response. In a post on X, the board said it is closely monitoring vulnerabilities identified in the portal operated by its service provider after concerns were raised in the public domain.

Man accused of killing Ghaziabad teen on Bakrid shot dead in police encounterGhaziabad Police shot Surya Chauhan murder accused Asad during an attempted interception in Khora. The encounter has sharply escalated a case that had already triggered public outrage in the region.

Singapore Open Final Live: SatChi take the second game 21-17Singapore Open Final Live Updates: (Sat/Chi 1-1 Alfain/Fikri, Game 3) Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty go into the decider after losing the first game and winning the second against Indonesia's Fajar Alfian and Muhammad Fikri in the Singapore Open men's doubles final. The Indian pair booked their spot in the summit clash after a strong win over world champions from Republic of Korea, Kim Won-ho and Seo Seung-jae in straight sets.

At the bottom of sea? Iran Navy has a safe haven where US power hits a wallDonald Trump has mocked Iran's navy, saying US and Israeli strikes sent it to the bottom. But Iran has another navy that the US can't touch. It's stationed on Iran's northern coast, on the Caspian Sea. It continues to operate under a protected geopolitical shield. Here's everything you need to know about it.

17-year-old Soros agents: Rahul Gandhi jabs BJP during chat with Class 12 studentsRahul Gandhi shared a video of his interaction with CBSE Class 12 students questioning their Physics marks and re-evaluation. The exchange added to the wider row over answer sheet discrepancies, online abuse and scrutiny of the board's marking system.

Indians are buying homes with money they haven't earned yetAs home prices race ahead of incomes, more Indians are stretching EMIs into retirement, cutting savings and relying on decades of future earnings to keep the dream of homeownership alive. This is the story of how India's housing dream is increasingly being financed by tomorrow's income.

Parking dispute turns violent in Himachal's Kasol as tourist shoots localA parking dispute in Kasol market allegedly led a tourist to fire at a local youth. The incident triggered panic, led to four arrests, and renewed concerns over tourist behaviour.

How an Indian figured out a way to classify sound 17 centuries before the WestScience of Sound: Seventeen centuries before the German Hornbostel-Sachs system, India's Natya Shastra had already sorted every musical instrument by what vibrates inside it, and a Bengali scholar carried that science to Europe, which took the credit.

US-Iran deal in limbo as Trump walks out of key White House meetingAhead of the meeting, which was held on Friday, Trump had insisted in a social media post that a deal extending the cease-fire would involve the reopening of the conflict-hit Strait of Hormuz by Iran.

After Abhishek, Trinamool MP Kalyan Banerjee mobbed, injured near KolkataKalyan Banerjee was injured during a clash outside Chanditala police station as TMC supporters were carrying out a protest march. The confrontation drew police and central forces, and Banerjee called it a targeted attack on a democratic protest.

True friendship developing: US defence chief's praise for Asim Munir, PakistanPete Hegseth said the US does not currently regard India or Pakistan as a missile threat. His remarks highlighted Washington's effort to deepen ties with both countries amid shifting South Asian security dynamics.

The real horror in Barker's Obsession isn't supernatural but male entitlementCurry Barker's Obsession follows Bear's wish to make Nikki love him above all else. The film uses that supernatural premise to probe consent, agency and self-serving desire.

Delhi-NCR to get fierce rain tonight. Will it bring a winter-like chill?An unusually intense Western Disturbance is moving across Northern India, triggering severe weather warnings. Delhi-NCR and Haryana must brace for sudden thunderstorms, heavy rain and lightning tonight.

Sexting, half-naked photos on hookup app shake US Democrat's Senate campaignworGraham Platner appeared in a towel-clad selfie linked to his Kik account, an app often associated with hook-up messaging. The image shows his upper body and several tattoos, though his face is not visible. The photograph appears to have been taken in a bathroom mirror.

Saheb, I have a passport, why don't I still feel sufficiently Indian?Perhaps that is the paradox of citizenship in current India: the more documents one gathers to prove belonging, the farther one drifts from the feeling of truly belonging.

CBSE OSM row exposes a hard truth: Class 12 marks still matterThe CBSE Class 12 marksheet may no longer decide a student's entire future, but it remains powerful enough to trigger panic when trust in the process breaks down. As a mother of a 16-year-old headed for the 2027 boards, I have to ask, why does an exam we claim matters less still hold such a chokehold on us?
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

Video: Mamata Banerjee berates hospital CEO after 'bhaipo' attackedTMC leader Abhishek Banerjee was attacked during his visit to Sonarpur near Kolkata, where he had gone to meet the families of alleged victims of post-poll violence. Scores of people hurled stones, eggs and abuses at the TMC national general secretary.

Jana Nayagan director on film delay: I don't have the courage to reveal the truthDirector H Vinoth gave a cryptic answer when asked about Jana Nayagan's release during a promotional interview. Touted to be Thalapathy Vijay's final film, Jana Nayagan is rumoured to hit theatres on the actor-politician's 52nd birthday in June.

The FIFA World Cup is coming. India still doesn't know where to watch itEvery four years, the FIFA World Cup turns India into a football-crazy nation, regardless of whether the national team is playing. Yet with kickoff around the corner, millions of fans found themselves waiting not for fixtures, but for a broadcaster.

Can attack on Abhishek Banerjee become Mamata's next political turning point?The attack on Abhishek Banerjee has triggered an unexpected show of support for Mamata Banerjee from across the Opposition. The response has revived questions over whether a battered Trinamool Congress can still help pull together the fractured INDIA bloc.

In 1997, India's ocean saved the monsoon from El Nino. This year, it won'tIn 1997, one of the strongest El Ninos ever recorded could not break India's monsoon because the Indian Ocean pushed back. The IOD is flat this year. El Nino is still building. And the September peak is heading straight for India's standing crops.

Man repeatedly slams toddler to ground, kills him to marry child's motherThe accused killed the one-and-a-half-year-old boy by repeatedly smashing him onto the ground, as he considered the toddler an obstacle to his relationship with the child's mother.

sports.headline: IPL final live: RCB chase dynasty status, GT eye perfect revenge at home IPL Final 2026, Bengaluru (RCB) vs Gujarat (GT) Live Score: Two years ago, RCB were cricket's favourite punchline. Tonight, they can take another giant step towards becoming an IPL powerhouse. Standing in their way: a wounded, dangerous Gujarat Titans.

Sachin Tendulkar shares the ultimate wishlist to reshape modern IPL cricket Sachin Tendulkar has called for the IPL's Impact Player rule to be scrapped, saying it unfairly favours batters. He also proposed a bowling powerplay and a five-over quota for one bowler.

Singapore Open Final Live: atChi hold advantage in second game (11-8) after losing first game Singapore Open Final Live Updates: (Sat/Chi 16-14 Alfain/Fikri, Game 2) Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty lose the first game 18-21 to Indonesia's Fajar Alfian and Muhammad Fikri in the Singapore Open men's doubles final. The Indian pair booked their spot in the summit clash after a strong win over world champions from Republic of Korea, Kim Won-ho and Seo Seung-jae in straight sets.

An IPL trophy could be Shubman GIll's T20I ticket. Could be IPL 2026: Three IPL seasons, nearly 2,000 runs, and two World Cup snubs. He might win an IPL title as captain on Sunday. Shubman Gill has made the loudest possible case for a T20I recall. Whether India's selectors are listening is an entirely different question.

PM Modi on Mann ki Baat talks track rivalry with Gurindervir Singh and Animesh Kujur PM Modi praised Gurindervir Singh and Animesh Kujur for their record-breaking sprint feats, highlighting how the close friends push each other on the track while helping Indian athletics grow.

Sree Charani backs India to bounce back ahead of T20 World Cup after ENG loss Women's T20 World Cup: Sree Charani has backed India to respond in Taunton after England's 26-run win in Bristol levelled the Women's T20I series. Her three wickets and calm appraisal underline how closely India are weighing form, temperament and bowling balance before the World Cup.

The grace of lift: Pooja Singh and the art of the high jump Pooja Singh's historic 1.93m high jump to win Asian U20 gold did more than shatter India's senior national record. Her journey from practicing on village rice sacks to mastering visual grace on the world stage redefines the beauty of flight.

Luis Enrique eyes more Champions League crowns: 'I can do four' after PSG win PSG beat Arsenal on penalties in Budapest to retain the Champions League under Luis Enrique. The win sealed back-to-back European titles and drew praise from Mikel Arteta and Nasser Al-Khelaifi.

Can Virat Kohli strike gold? Rashid Khan a big concern: Key stats for IPL 2026 final IPL 2026, RCB vs GT: Rajat Patidar's Royal Challengers Bengaluru and Shubman Gill's Gujarat Titans have all to play for when they lock horns in the grand finale on Sunday, May 31 at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad.

Sachin Tendulkar's timeless advice for rising star Sooryavanshi after defeat vs GT IPL 2026: Sachin Tendulkar has urged Vaibhav Sooryavanshi to stay true to his natural game amid mounting India talk. The advice captures both the scale of the 15-year-old's rise and the need to shield him from premature pressure.

Roland Garros Day 8 Order of Play: Djoker-slayer Fonseca back for 4th round and more Day 8 of Roland Garros brings massive fourth-round matches to Paris. Top seeds Alexander Zverev and Iga Swiatek look to dominate, while teen star João Fonseca faces Casper Ruud under the lights.

The FIFA World Cup is coming. India still doesn't know where to watch itEvery four years, the FIFA World Cup turns India into a football-crazy nation, regardless of whether the national team is playing. Yet with kickoff around the corner, millions of fans found themselves waiting not for fixtures, but for a broadcaster.

IPL final preview: Red-hot RCB chase era-defining sequel in AhmedabadIPL 2026, RCB vs GT Preview: A perfectly calibrated RCB and a battle-hardened Gujarat Titans clash in Ahmedabad. With historical legacy on the line, the IPL 2026 grand finale promises an era-defining sequel to crown cricket's new aristocracy.

PSG fans set fires, scuffle with police after Champions League title win: WatchPSG retained their Champions League title after beating Arsenal on penalties in Budapest, while celebrations in Paris were marred by clashes with police, fires and more than 130 arrests.

An IPL champion already, Virat Kohli remains the soul of RCB's title defenceIPL Final, RCB vs GT: Virat Kohli no longer needs an IPL trophy to complete his story. He already has one. Yet as Royal Challengers Bengaluru prepare for another title-defining night, Virat Kohli remains the emotional pulse of a franchise that has grown with him.

PSG break Arsenal hearts to retain Champions League crownArsenal were minutes away from completing a dream season with a first-ever Champions League title. Instead, PSG held their nerve in a penalty shootout to retain their European crown and leave the Gunners' long wait for continental glory intact.

Defending champion Coco Gauff crashes out as Potapova delivers French Open shockDefending champion Coco Gauff's French Open title defence came to a stunning end on Saturday as Austria's Anastasia Potapova battled back from a set down to secure a 4-6, 7-6(1), 6-4 victory and advance to the fourth round at Roland Garros.

Vinesh Phogat's return to wrestling: What a messVinesh Phogat returned to the wrestling mat for the first time since the Paris Olympics, hoping to secure her place at the 2026 Asian Games. Instead, her comeback became a day-long confrontation with the Wrestling Federation of India.

Freya Kemp powers England past India to level women's T20I seriesFreya Kemp starred with bat and ball as England beat India by 26 runs in the second Women's T20I in Bristol on Saturday to level the three-match series 1-1. The series-deciding third T20I will be played in Taunton on Tuesday.

IPL 2026 final: Gujarat's Ahmedabad arrival delayed until Sat eve due to stormA day before the IPL 2026 final, Gujarat Titans were forced to alter their travel plans after stormy weather delayed their departure from Chandigarh, leaving them with little time to settle in Ahmedabad ahead of Sunday's clash against Royal Challengers Bengaluru.

RCB vs GT: Gujarat Titans' Shubman Gill looks to exploit home advantage in IPL finalIPL 2026 Final, RCB vs GT: Standing on the verge of winning his first IPL title as captain, Gujarat Titans skipper Shubman Gill said he is not weighed down by expectations and believes his side can benefit from playing the final at home against Royal Challengers Bengaluru on Sunday.

UCL Final Highlights: PSG beat Arsenal 4-3 on penalties to win Champions LeaguePSG vs Arsenal Highlights, UEFA Champions League Final: PSG have beaten Arsenal to defend their Champions League title. Gabriel's penalty miss hands PSG the title, and makes Luis Enrique a 3-time UCL winning manager.

Rajat Patidar reveals secret behind RCB's push for consecutive IPL titlesRajat Patidar has revealed the captaincy mantra behind Royal Challengers Bengaluru's success as they stand just one win away from retaining their IPL title. While victory over Gujarat Titans in Sunday's final would see him join MS Dhoni and Rohit Sharma as the only captains to successfully defend an IPL crown, Patidar said he has always preferred to lead in his own way rather than follow the path set by others.

Sabalenka powers past Kasatkina, books fourth-round date with OsakaAryna Sabalenka continued her strong run at the French Open, beating Australia's Daria Kasatkina 6-0, 7-5 on Saturday to reach the fourth round at Roland Garros and set up a blockbuster clash with four-time Grand Slam champion Naomi Osaka.

Vinesh Phogat stands proud after Asian Games trials: Fighting the whole systemDefiant despite missing an Asian Games berth, Vinesh Phogat declared she won her true victory by overcoming administrative hurdles and returning to the mat as a mother. The former world medalist slammed the wrestling system while confirming her target remains the 2028 Olympics.

Will the IPL final answer what Gill and Patidar won't about T20I selection?On the eve of the IPL 2026 final, RCB's Rajat Patidar stonewalled and GT's Shubman Gill chose his words carefully. Both have made their case with the bat when it comes to T20I selection chatter Now the biggest game of the year must do the rest.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is world's best batter right now: Shubman GillIPL 2026: Gujarat Titans captain Shubman Gill called Rajasthan Royals wonderkid Vaibhav Sooryavanshi the world's best batter right now after their Qualifier 2 clash. Despite hitting a winning century himself, Gill was left in absolute awe of the 15-year-old's fearless mindset.

Singapore Open: Satwik-Chirag stun world no. 1 Seo-Kim to enter finalSatwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty produced a superb display to beat World No. 1 pair Seo Seungjae and Kim Wonho in straight games and reach the men's doubles final of the Singapore Open on Saturday.

Liverpool sack head coach Arne Slot after dismal Premier League title defenceLiverpool have sacked Arne Slot after a disappointing 2025-26 season that saw the club finish fifth in the Premier League. The Dutchman departs just a year after leading the Reds to the league title in his debut campaign at Anfield.

Main wapas aaungi: Vinesh exits Asian Games trials after semi-final defeatVinesh Phogat was knocked out of the Asian Games selection trials here on Saturday after losing to Meenakshi Goyat 4-6 in a tense semifinal bout of the women's 53kg event.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
